data.title: Jeffs' Brands: KeepZone AI Enters into Agent Agreement with Skyline Software Systems to Offer 3D Geospatial Visualization Solutions to Global Security Markets
data.description: Tel Aviv, Israel- March 27, 2026- Jeffs' Brands Ltd, a data-driven e-commerce company traded as Nasdaq: JFBR and JFBRW, is venturing into the global homeland security industry with AI-driven solutions. The company's subsidiary, KeepZone AI Inc., has recently signed a non-exclusive Agent Agreement with Skyline Software Systems, a prominent provider of 3D geospatial visualization services. This partnership aims to enhance Jeffs' Brands Ltd's offerings in homeland security, leveraging Skyline's expertise in the field.

data.title: Meta Stock: What's Next After 'Big Tobacco Moment' Fears Fuel Sell-Off?
data.description: Following court losses, Meta's market cap plummeted by $119 billion. Investors are now wondering about the company's next steps after concerns of a potential 'Big Tobacco Moment'. The post discusses the implications of these events on Meta Stock.

data.title: Arcadia University Named Top Producer of Gilman Scholars Over 25 Years
data.description: Arcadia University has been recognized as a top producer in the Gilman Program's 25th Anniversary, with 81 of its students receiving merit-based Gilman awards for study or internships abroad since 2001. The university, located in Glenside, Pennsylvania, is renowned for its national study abroad programs.

data.title: Bitcoin drops under $67k amid war worries as Fannie Mae accepts cryptocurrency for mortgages.
data.description: The price of Bitcoin dropped below $67,000 amid rising concerns of war tensions. In a significant development, Fannie Mae has decided to permit the use of cryptocurrency in the mortgage process.

data.title: AstraZeneca Strengthens Position In COPD Race With Successful Trials
data.description: AstraZeneca Plc recently shared encouraging results from Phase 3 OBERON and TITANIA trials investigating tozorakimab in chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) patients. COPD, commonly known as smoker's lung disease, was targeted with this monoclonal antibody aimed at interleukin-33 (IL-33). By inhibiting IL-33 signaling, tozorakimab can potentially lower inflammation levels and disrupt the mucus dysfunction cycle worsening COPD symptoms. Data demonstrated a decrease in the annualized rate of moderate-to-severe COPD exacerbations with tozorakimab compared to a placebo. The primary endpoint was met in both the main group of former smokers and the overall population consisting of former and current smokers, across all blood eosinophil counts and disease stages. More details are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Camden Property Trust Announces Promotions and Changes to Senior Leadership Team
data.description: In Houston, Texas, Camden Property Trust (NYSE:CPT) revealed new promotions in its senior leadership team. Alexander J. Jessett is now the Chief Executive Officer and a member of Camden's Board of Trust Managers. Laurie A. Baker has been promoted to President and Chief Operating Officer. Benjamin D. Fraker is now the Executive Vice President, Chief Financial Officer, and Treasurer. Richard J. Campo, the Co-Founder, also experienced changes in his role within the company.

data.title: Callan JMB Supports U.S. Scale-Up of Multi-Indication Immune Platform with Phase 2b/3 Advancement
data.description: An external review is endorsing the integrity of manufacturing processes, supply chain management, and federal preparedness for a multi-indication platform. This oversight is crucial for ensuring the quality of processes and the readiness to expand across various uses.

data.title: Axe Compute to Host Annual Report Conference Call
data.description: Axe Compute is set to announce its financial results for the fiscal year 2025 and submit its Annual Report on Form 10-K. The company has scheduled a conference call and webcast for April 1, 2026, to discuss these results and other pertinent information. Investors and stakeholders are encouraged to participate in the virtual event to gain insights into Axe Compute's performance over the past year. This disclosure is a crucial step in maintaining transparency and keeping shareholders informed about the company's financial health and strategic direction.

data.title: China's SMIC Reportedly Supplied Chipmaking Tools To Iran's Military— A Stance In The War?
data.description: China's largest semiconductor manufacturer, SMIC, is said to have supplied chipmaking equipment to Iran's military, sparking tensions between the U.S. and China. According to two senior officials from the Trump administration, the tools were sent to Iran's armed forces. It remains unclear whether the tools were of U.S. origin, potentially violating U.S. sanctions. The cooperation likely involved technical training on semiconductor technology. The tools were allegedly provided to Iran's "military industrial complex" and could be utilized for various electronic purposes. This development comes during a critical time in the U.S.-Iran conflict. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: VERSES® Announces Closing of Second Tranche of Private Placement Offering of Units
data.description: VERSES AI Inc., a cognitive computing company based in Vancouver, completed a second tranche of its non-brokered private placement by issuing 473,500 units at a price of C$0.75 (US$0.54) per Unit, generating gross proceeds of C$355,125 (approximately US$257,318). Together with the first tranche, the company has now raised a total of C$1,100,930 (approximately US$797,717) through the issuance of 1,644,307 Units, also eliminating C$132,300 (approximately US$95,863) in liabilities. This places VERSES in a stronger financial position and marks a significant milestone for the Company.
